tick_queue_approved.name: ip-cidr
tick_queue_approved.idea: IP/CIDR Calculator & Subnet Analyzer — network address, broadcast, mask, wildcard, host count, binary bitmask; Is-IP-in-range checker; subnet splitter; bulk comparison
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Cloud/backend/devops engineers deal with CIDR blocks every day. Pure 32-bit bitwise integer math in JS, zero deps.

tick_queue_approved.name: port-ref
tick_queue_approved.idea: Well-Known Port Quick-Reference — type a port or service name, get IANA service, protocol, privilege boundary warning (<1024), security flag; bulk annotation from docker-compose / k8s manifests
tick_queue_approved.rationale: DevOps engineers consult port numbers constantly. Pure JS static lookup, PWA, tight scope.

resume_instructions: TESTS gate escalation (attempt 2) on ip-cidr. IMPLEMENTATION gate resolved (overlap logic fixed, CSP hash replaced). One remaining objection: UI (medium): Ctrl+Click to copy binary is not discoverable — add a visible Copy button next to the binary display (#bin-display). The button should copy the current binary string to clipboard (same logic as Ctrl+Click). Place it inline after the bin-display div, styled as a small .btn. After adding the button, recompute the SHA-256 hash of the <script> block and update the CSP meta tag. Then clear council_escalations and re-run: python3 council.py --gate tests --project ip-cidr --goal "IP/CIDR Calculator & Subnet Analyzer — network address, broadcast, mask, wildcard, host count, binary bitmask; Is-IP-in-range checker; subnet splitter; bulk comparison" --context ip-cidr/index.html --inline "pre-filter results: test_project PASS, eval_bugs PASS, security_scan PASS" --attempt 1

council_escalations.reason: UI discoverability: Ctrl+Click binary copy has no visible affordance — council wants a Copy button next to the binary display.
